title: Tauschia alpina
intro: Tauschia alpina, also known as Mountain Carrot or Western Yampah, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Tauschia alpina include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Tauschia alpina is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Tauschia alpina is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is often classified as a perennial herb. Tauschia alpina is commonly referred to as Alpina or Tauschia. Its classification is further divided into various sub-species, each with distinct characteristics.
uses: Tauschia alpina, also known as alpine desert parsley, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
